mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS of at least 100,000 persons predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by at least 100,000 persons month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:March 2016 payroll growth of 215,000 persons signals labor market resilience
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with sustained job creation
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS surprises (>100k monthly gains) fail to produce positive 20d forward returns in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains at historically low levels (near zero), indicating a prolonged monetary accommodation regime. This sustained low-rate environment compresses equity risk premiums and supports equity valuations. The mechanism is always-on when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5 percent per annum.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.36% as of March 2016 reflects post-crisis accommodation
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction sector demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this indicates broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both proxies to move in the same direction.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:February 2016 housing starts at 1,178k and retail sales at $446.9B indicate demand resilience
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in HOUST and RSAFS fail to produce positive 20d forward returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic output capacity utilization. A sustained decline in INDPRO signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential economic slack, which typically precedes equity market weakness as growth expectations compress. A 3-month rolling decline in INDPRO predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.38 in March 2016 shows recent weakness in manufacturing
failure_condition: Three-month INDPRO declines fail to produce negative 5d forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year signals monetary tightening pressure and real rate compression concerns. When inflation accelerates, the Federal Reserve faces pressure to raise rates, which increases equity discount rates and reduces forward valuations. This mechanism activates when CPI growth exceeds 2.5% annualized.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.92 in March 2016 reflects subdued inflation environment
regime_hint: inflation acceleration or monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: CPI acceleration above 2.5% fails to produce negative 60d forward returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure labor market tightness. When UNRATE falls while PAYEMS rises simultaneously, this signals accelerating labor demand that compresses unemployment and supports wage growth expectations, driving equity valuations higher over 20 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines month-over-month AND PAYEMS increases month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% and PAYEMS at 143,774k in March 2016 show stable labor market
regime_hint: tight labor market with synchronized employment gains and unemployment decline
failure_condition: Joint signals of falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS fail to produce positive 20d forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly represents the broadest measure of economic demand. Quarters with positive real GDP growth above 2% annualized signal sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations. This mechanism is always-on and predicts positive 60-day forward returns when the most recent quarterly GDP growth exceeds 2%.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at $16,470.6B in Q3 2015 reflects moderate economic expansion
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth above 2% annualized
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth above 2% fail to produce positive 60d forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates real rate compression. When real rates become deeply negative (FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL growth rate), equity valuations expand as investors seek yield in equities. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns when real rates compress below zero.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 3-month annualized CPIAUCSL growth rate
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.36% versus subdued CPI growth creates negative real rates
regime_hint: post-crisis accommodation with negative real rates
failure_condition: Negative real rate periods fail to produce positive 20d forward returns in more than 58% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
